info.title: boots
info.description: boots is a dotnet global tool for "bootstrapping" vsix & pkg files.

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: ArgumentExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Parse, OK: OptionExtensions.Parse, OK: SymbolExtensions.Parse, OK: Parser.Parse, OK: ParserExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Invoke, OK: CommandExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParseResultExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParserExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParserExtensions.Invoke, OK: CommandExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: CommandExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParseResultExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParserExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParserExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ModelBindingCommandHandler.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

options.name: url
options.description: A URL to a pkg or vsix file to install

options.name: stable
options.description: Install the latest *stable* version of a product from VS manifests. Options include: Xamarin.Android, Xamarin.iOS, Xamarin.Mac, and Mono.

options.name: preview
options.description: Install the latest *preview* version of a product from VS manifests. Options include: Xamarin.Android, Xamarin.iOS, Xamarin.Mac, and Mono.

options.name: alpha
options.description: Install the latest *alpha* version of a product from VS manifests. This is only valid for Visual Studio for Mac. Options include: Xamarin.Android, Xamarin.iOS, Xamarin.Mac, and Mono.

options.name: file-type
options.description: Specifies the type of file to be installed such as vsix, pkg, or msi. Defaults to vsix on Windows and pkg on macOS.

options.name: timeout
options.description: Specifies a timeout for HttpClient. If omitted, uses the .NET default of 100 seconds.

options.name: read-write-timeout
options.description: Specifies a timeout for reading/writing from a HttpClient stream. If omitted, uses a default of 300 seconds.

options.name: retries
options.description: Specifies a number of retries for HttpClient failures. If omitted, uses a default of 3 retries.

options.name: downgrade-first
options.description: Downgrades the existing vsix back to the shipped VS default before install. This allows an earlier vsix to be installed.
